thesis: Franco-Nevada is the world's largest gold-focused royalty and streaming company, offering asset-light exposure to precious metals without operational or debt risk. Record Q1 2026 results ($650.7M revenue, +77% YoY) demonstrate leverage to gold prices above $3,000/oz. Zero debt, 19 consecutive dividend increases, and a diversified portfolio of 400+ assets provide a fortress balance sheet. Key catalysts include Cobre Panamá arbitration (Oct 2026), new stream acquisitions, and continued central bank gold demand.

drivers: Gold sustains $3,200+/oz on central bank buying and geopolitical risk premium
Cobre Panamá arbitration delivers partial or full $5B recovery
New acquisitions (Casa Berardi, i-80 Gold) add incremental GEOs

risks: Gold drops below $2,500/oz on rate hikes or risk-on sentiment
Cobre Panamá arbitration fails; asset permanently impaired
Miners cut production at key assets due to lower metal prices
Multiple compression to 18x P/E on lower earnings

base_case.assumptions: Gold averages $3,000/oz through 2026–2027; GEO production hits mid-point of guidance (~540K oz); Cobre Panamá remains unresolved but stockpile deliveries offset some loss; modest multiple compression offset by earnings growth.

key_risks: Gold price volatility and macro sensitivity
Geopolitical risk in Latin American operating jurisdictions
Interest rate environment reducing dividend stock attractiveness
